beats.narration: Inside every ultrasonic probe sits one thin disc that both transmits the pulse and receives the echo — speaker and microphone in one.

payload.title: THE PIEZO ELEMENT
payload.sub: One thin disc is both loudspeaker and microphone.

beats.narration: Voltage in, the disc changes thickness — the inverse effect transmits. Echo pressure in, the disc makes voltage — the direct effect receives.
Quartz was rugged but inefficient. P Z T became the workhorse — efficient both ways. Piezocomposites add sensitivity and bandwidth for phased array.
The element resonates when it's half a wavelength thick: frequency equals velocity divided by twice the thickness. Thinner element, higher frequency — and more fragile.
One rule protects every probe: heat the element past its Curie point, even once, and it's permanently dead. Keep standard probes off hot surfaces.

payload.sub: Keep standard probes off hot surfaces.
